content:
type: REVISE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Both our features need core.py's Option class, which risks real textual collisions if we edit it in parallel. Proposal: I own core.py entirely (dynamic default resolution touches Option.__init__, get_default/process_value, and Command.main second-pass resolution, plus help text). You own types.py entirely for batch_size. If your batch_size feature genuinely needs core.py changes (e.g. a new Option param), tell me the exact minimal addition (param name/signature) and I'll fold it in as part of my edit pass so we don't clobber each other - or we serialize: I finish + DONE, then you edit core.py after. Let me know which you prefer.

content:
type: DONE
my_files: src/click/core.py
your_files: src/click/types.py
note: Finished dynamic-default-resolution. Ended up only touching src/click/core.py (no types.py changes needed) - added Option._dynamic_default detection/validation in Option.__init__, a get_default() short-circuit to None for dynamic defaults, (dynamic) help-text display in get_help_extra, and a Command._resolve_dynamic_defaults() second-pass hooked into Command.make_context() after parse_args. All existing tests pass (680 passed, pre-existing unrelated collection error in test_basic.py on base commit too, due to a pytest-version deprecation, not from my changes). Patch written to patch.txt in my own sandbox - since core.py wasn't shared/visible between us (commit 1ebb256 never appeared here), my diff is purely additive against 5c1239b and shouldn't conflict with yours at merge time.
